title: TypeDefinition (v2 stdlib derivation)
description: Declares a named type that the engine accumulates memory around. Each TypeDefinition manifest binds a name to a memory-governance policy: how grammar (how this type composes with others) and lexicon (specific instance content + outcome signals) are tracked, retained, and decayed. v061 ships the authoring surface and the empty grammar/lexicon tables; v062 wires the consolidation pipeline that fills them; v063 lights up concept stabilization on top. Derivation: compose(Entity[type_identity], Policy[memory_governance]).

type_kind.description: Coarse classification driving default memory-policy values: 'domain' (long-lived concepts, grammar emphasis), 'process' (action templates, procedural emphasis), 'event' (timestamped occurrences, lexicon emphasis), 'relation' (typed edges, both).

memory_block.description: How memory accumulates for instances of this type. All fields optional; sensible defaults vary by type_kind.

decay_half_life_days.description: Half-life in days for grammar cell decay. Grammar tracks 'how this type composes' — typically slow decay (months).

retention_days.description: Hard retention window for instance rows. Older rows get archived (or distilled into grammar via consolidation).

max_rows.description: Soft cap on rows-per-type-per-scope. Older rows beyond this drop on next consolidation pass even if within retention_days.

reinforcement.description: Thresholds that trigger promotion from observation -> reinforced template (procedural memory) or lexicon -> grammar (semantic memory).

concept_stabilization.description: v063+ — concept-stabilization thresholds. Set enabled=true once v063 ships; v061/v062 ignore the block.

min_branch_diversity.description: Number of distinct scopes that must reinforce before a concept is proposed for stabilization (per Chomskian R18).

type_namespace.description: Optional dotted namespace. Defaults to the OU's slug-path when omitted (e.g. an OU at /acme/legal yields namespace 'acme.legal'). Prevents cross-branch type-name collisions.

applies_to_scope_ref.description: Scope inside which this type is recognized (e.g. 'legal.contracts'). Memory cells key on (type, scope_id) so the same type name can carry different grammar/lexicon in different scopes.

extends_type_ref.description: Optional parent TypeDefinition name. Inherited types share grammar (read-up; child cells are deltas) but have independent lexicon. v062's query API surfaces the merged grammar.
